basics.name: Anne Gracia A
basics.label: Chemical Engineering Graduate & Materials Researcher
basics.summary: Chemical Engineering graduate and Materials Researcher with a strong foundation in electrochemistry, process optimization, and computational workflows. Experienced in bridging experimental material characterization with Python/MATLAB-driven data analysis. Seeking Master's programs in Materials, Energy, or Chemical Engineering at German universities for Summer 2027.

work.name: N-ERGY AI Solutions India Pvt Ltd
highlights: Building automated literature ingestion pipeline using OpenAlex API for MatGeek RAG agent
Developing Plotly-based data visualization interfaces for simulation results
Collaborating with ML and backend teams on predictive workflows and research-oriented platform features
Developing and deploying Python UQ classes to standardize automated analysis of complex materials datasets

work.name: N-ERGY AI Solutions India Pvt Ltd
highlights: Optimized Aluminum thin-film deposition using RF sputtering to achieve uniform 100 nm films for microdevice architectures
Experienced in materials synthesis through sol-gel and metallurgical routes
Optimized industrial electrochemical anodizing processes for automotive components
Improved surface microfinish by 78% and mitigated mass transport/diffusion defects within the electrolyte bath

volunteer.organization: Pallavaram Tanners Industrial Effluent Treatment Co. Ltd.
highlights: Studied leather tanning and industrial effluent treatment processes
Gained exposure to environmental engineering and wastewater management
Observed operational and strategic aspects of food processing industry
Applied chemical engineering principles to process optimization and quality control

projects.name: Enhancing Microfinish and Resolving Smut and Powdery Coating Issues in the Anodizing Process for Automotive Plunger Manufacturing
highlights: Optimized industrial electrochemical anodizing processes for automotive aluminium plungers
Improved surface microfinish by 78% and mitigated mass transport/diffusion defects
Optimized oxide layer thickness to TOL = 25.54 μm using RSM (Minitab)
Validated corrosion resistance using EIS, potentiodynamic polarization, and Salt Spray Test (ASTM B117)

projects.name: Pitting Corrosion Behaviour of WAAM 316L Stainless Steel
highlights: Investigated electrochemical kinetics and pitting corrosion behavior of WAAM 316L stainless steel using potentiodynamic polarization and microscopy
Compared as-printed and heat-treated conditions (HTT1–HTT4)
Project funded with 35,000 INR — Guide: Dr. A.K. Lakshminarayanan
Developed Python data processing and uncertainty quantification (UQ) classes

projects.name: RF Sputtering of Aluminium Thin Films for Metal-Induced Crystallization
highlights: Deposited ~100 nm Al thin films on Si wafers using RF sputtering for quantum computing applications
Achieved fine uniform grains (~40 nm) at room temperature deposition (250 W, 0.001 mbar)
Characterized using FESEM, AFM (Ra = 5.385 nm), and thickness profilometry
Target application: Metal-Induced Layer Exchange (MIL) for isotopically enriched Si-28
